channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Nick Pickles Joins Forces with Sam Altman to Tackle AI-Powered Online Threats
item.description: Nick Pickles joins Tools for Humanity, a company building technology to support World Network, as chief policy officer. He aims to address the growing threat of AI-powered online attacks, particularly those targeting human identity verification.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: AI discrimination lawsuit reaches $2.2 million settlement
item.description: Mary Louis’s excitement to move into an apartment in Massachusetts in the spring of 2021 turned to dismay when Louis, a Black woman, received an email saying that a “third-party service” had denied her tenancy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Eric Schmidt on Henry Kissinger’s surprising warning to the world on AI
item.description: Humans are beginning to really reckon with the possibility of sharing our planet with some far smarter non-corporeal entities.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Silicon Valley tech boom lifts California’s dreary budget view
item.description: A gauge of the so-called Magnificent Seven mega tech companies — Alphabet, Amazon.com Inc., Apple, Meta, Microsoft Corp., Nvidia and Tesla Inc. — is up 56% so far this year, more than twice the advance of the S&P 500 Index.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Bezos pushes back on Musk claim he said Trump would lose
item.description: Jeff Bezos is pushing back on a claim from fellow billionaire Elon Musk that the Amazon founder and Washington Post owner predicted former President Trump would lose this month's presidential election to Vice President Harris.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: US Justice Department seeks Google Chrome sale to curb monopoly
item.description: The US Justice Department and a group of states proposed major changes to Alphabet Inc.’s Google — including a forced sale of the company’s Chrome web browser — after a landmark ruling that the tech giant illegally monopolized online search.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Why Cloud Economics is the answer to the AI innovation/cost conundrum
item.description: Cloud economics enables AI innovation by aligning cloud spending with business value, optimizing costs through FinOps.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: FCC chair to step down on Trump's inauguration
item.description: Federal Communications Commission (FCC) Chair Jessica Rosenworcel announced Wednesday she will depart the regulatory agency on Inauguration Day when President-elect Trump returns to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: A blueprint for successfully executing business-aligned IT strategies
item.description: In our fast-changing digital world, it’s essential to sync IT strategies with business objectives for lasting success. Technology has shifted from a back-office function to a core enabler of business growth, innovation, and competitive advantage.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Thai court dismisses activist's suit against Israeli spyware producer over lack of evidence
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A Thai court on Thursday threw out a lawsuit brought by a pro-democracy activist which alleged spyware produced by an Israeli tech firm had been used to hack his phone.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AI agents are coming to work — here’s what businesses need to know
item.description: AI agents will soon be everywhere, automating complex business processes and taking care of mundane tasks for workers — at least that’s the claim of various software vendors that are quickly adding intelligent bots to a wide range of work apps. Many companies appear to be listening.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: CIOs view cost management as possible AI value killer
item.description: CIOs feeling the pressure to deploy successful AI projects have a second concern: that they don’t have the money to pull it off. Ninety percent of CIOs recently surveyed by Gartner say that managing AI costs is limiting their ability to get value from AI.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: La digitalización de la tarjeta de transporte público de Madrid, una realidad
item.description: Un reclamo social desde que la digitalización se convirtió en cotidianidad. Cualquier madrileño que se precie ha deseado alguna vez poder deshacerse de la tarjeta física de transporte público para evitar cargar sus bolsillos y acceder a la red del suburbano empleando su dispositivo móvil.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: FedEx CIO Rob Carter on building a world-class IT culture
item.description: Robert B. Carter is a visionary technology executive whose pioneering career and accomplishments have set the standard for IT leadership excellence.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Advanced programming with Java generics
item.description: Generics in Java enhance the type safety of your code and make it easier to read. In my last article, I introduced the general concepts of generics and showed examples from the Java Collections Framework. You also learned how to use generics to avoid runtime errors like the ClassCastException.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft 365 Copilot incorpora nuevos agentes de IA y automatiza tareas para mejorar flujos de trabajo
item.description: El gigante de Redmond, aprovechando el altavoz de su cita anual Microsoft Ignite 2024, ha anunciado nuevas funcionalidades en su cartera de soluciones.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Nvidia is Wall Street's most valuable company. How it got there, by the numbers
item.description: Nvidia has once again turned out quarterly results that exceeded Wall Street's forecasts. The company has seen soaring demand for its semiconductors, which are used to power artificial intelligence applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: You’ll soon be able to clone your voice to speak other languages ​​in Teams
item.description: In connection with this year’s Ignite conference, Microsoft has unveiled a new interpretation tool that will be added to Teams in the spring.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The Trump tariffs could create a huge headache for streamers
item.description: With Donald Trump set to return to the White House in January, streaming media and consumer electronics companies have to prepare for disruption ahead. Some of Trump’s policies could arguably benefit the industry.
item.dc:creator: Janko Roettgers, Fast Company

item.title: Google Docs makeover: How the word processor is getting smarter
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. Google Docs is getting smarter. New sub-pages help organize complex documents, AI can assist with editing, and pageless mode improves online reading.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft Ignite 2024 — get the latest news and insights
item.description: Microsoft Ignite 2024 kicks off in Chicago and runs Nov. 19-22. If you can’t make it to Chicago, no worries. First, the physical event is sold out, according to the Ignite event page. Second, it’s a hybrid event, so you can attend Ignite virtually.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: EU industry group has its eye on Microsoft’s cloud business
item.description: Microsoft’s cloud business is under surveillance in Europe: The association of Cloud Infrastructure Services Providers in Europe (CISPE) has set up new monitoring body to ensure the tech giant complies with a legal settlement to keep cloud licensing practices fair in the region.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Comcast to spin off cable networks that were once the entertainment giant's star performers
item.description: Comcast is spinning off into a new company many of its cable television networks that were once at the heart of the entertainment giant, as consumers increasingly swap out their cable TV subscriptions for streaming platforms.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Australia won't force social media users to share their personal details when child ban takes effect
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia's communications minister said Wednesday the government won't force social media users to hand over their personal information to tech companies, as children younger than 16 are set to be banned from the platforms.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: What is Rust? Safe, fast, and easy software development
item.description: A programming language can be fast, safe, or easy to write. As developers, we get to choose our priorities but we can only pick two. Programming languages that emphasize convenience and safety tend to be slow (like Python).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Germany blames ‘sabotage’ as two undersea fiber cables cut in the Baltic Sea
item.description: The German government has blamed an act of sabotage for the cutting of two important undersea fiber optic cables, one connecting connecting Finland and Germany, and the other linking Sweden and Lithuania, on Sunday and Monday. The first cable break happened at around 8 a.m. GMT (3 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Chat GPT-4 Outperforms Human Doctors in Disease Diagnosis Study
item.description: Artificial intelligence surpasses human expertise in medical diagnosis
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft pitches AI 'agents' that can perform tasks on their own at Ignite 2024
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella told customers at a conference in Chicago on Tuesday that the company is teaching a new set of artificial intelligence tools how to "act on our behalf across our work and life.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: HENNIKER, N.H. (AP) — When Hector Xu was learning to fly a helicopter in college, he recalled having a few “nasty experiences” while trying to navigate at night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A beautiful machine: one week with an M4 Pro MacBook Pro
item.description: The new MacBook Pro is everything you expect: faster, better and more capable than before. While you can say that about every new Mac, the move to Apple Silicon means Apple can introduce vastly improved systems almost every year — something that was not always possible before.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: German police break up an environmental activists' camp near Tesla plant outside Berlin
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German police broke up a protest camp housing environmental activists in a forest near the Tesla electric car factory in Grünheide outside Berlin on Tuesday, citing violations of public safety and order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Succeeding with observability in the cloud
item.description: In its 2024 report, Splunk breaks down observability practices into four stages: foundational visibility, guided insights, proactive response, and unified workflows. Based on that, it classifies companies into four stages of observability maturity: beginning, emerging, evolving, and leading.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: F# 9 adds nullable reference types
item.description: F# 9, the latest version of Microsoft’s open source functional language, has been released with support for nullable reference types and empty-bodied computation expressions. Standard library improvements also are featured, including random sampling functions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How ATO as code can reduce this compliance burden
item.description: OPM’s Dan Jacobs and stackArmor’s Gaurav “GP” Pal led an ACT-IAC team to develop a maturity model to help implement authority to operate as code. The post How ATO as code can reduce this compliance burden first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: PG&E blames birds for Tri-Valley power outages
item.description: According to the council’s agenda report filed ahead of Tuesday’s meeting, PG&E blamed birds for disrupting the region’s power supply.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Martin, The Mercury News

item.title: Company affiliated with Alex Jones seeks to disqualify The Onion's auction bid on Infowars
item.description: A company affiliated with conspiracy theorist Alex Jones asked a federal judge on Monday to disqualify a bid by the satirical news outlet The Onion to buy Jones' Infowars at a bankruptcy auction, alleging fraud and collusion.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: El Capitan bumps Frontier to claim world’s fastest supercomputer title
item.description: The new El Capitan system at the Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory in California made its debut at No. 1 on the latest TOP500 list of the world’s most powerful supercomputers, released today. The Frontier system at Oak Ridge National Laboratory in Tennessee moved down to the No.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Types of Preprocessors in Programming: A Guide
item.description: Learn about the different types of preprocessors, including code conversion, linting, documentation, and integrated data reporting, and how AI can improve preprocessing.
item.dc:creator: Info World
